
# General Requirements

## E.G.1: macOS Target
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application must target macOS (OS X) as the primary supported platform.

## E.G.2: Electron App Initialization
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Initialize a new Electron application with a working main process and renderer.

## E.G.3: Default Window
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


On launch, open a single main window with a default size and title.

## E.G.4: Local Dev Scripts
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Provide npm scripts to run the app in development mode on macOS.

## E.G.5: Minimal UI (Removed)
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

This requirement has been removed.

## E.G.6: Electron Version
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Use Electron v40.0.0 as the required version for this project.

## E.G.7: npm Version
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Use npm v11.8.0 as the required package manager version.

## E.G.8: Node.js Version
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

Use Node.js v25.5.0 as the required runtime version for this project.

## E.G.9: Fullscreen on Launch
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->

On launch, the main application window must open maximized, but not in fullscreen mode.

## E.G.10: App Name
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


The application name must be "Clerkly".

## E.G.11: TypeScript Codebase
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


All application code must be written in TypeScript.

## E.G.12: UI Reference Source
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


A Figma-generated UI reference is stored in docs/development/ui_reference and must be used as the visual reference for the app interface.

## E.G.13: Local SQLite Storage
<!--embedding:paraphrase-MiniLM-L3-v2:-->


Application data must be stored in a local SQLite database. If the database file is missing, the application must create it. If a database exists but its schema is not current, the application must run migrations to bring it to the latest schema. A backup of the existing database must be created before any migration is executed.
